definition: G7 fiscal positions have diverged to post-WWII extremes: US ~120% debt/GDP with 6-7% deficits, Japan ~260%, Italy ~140%, France ~115% (rising), UK ~100%, Germany ~65%. This creates the widest investable dispersion in sovereign risk across developed markets in modern history.
evidence: Directly observable from national statistics offices
Divergent trajectories (not just levels) across G7
Empirical observation confirmed at 9/10 across debates
open_questions: Does simultaneous G7 deterioration create a 'nowhere else to go' paradox that paradoxically supports each sovereign's funding?
Will divergence narrow or widen over the next 5 years?
What triggers capital reallocation based on fiscal divergence?
